channel.title: Asymmetric Intelligence
channel.description: A public intelligence commons. Eight monitors. One weekly briefing each.

item.title: European Strategic Autonomy Monitor — 16 May 2026
item.description: EEAS 4th FIMI Threat Report operationalises EU deterrence posture against Russian and Chinese information manipulation infrastructure

item.title: Strategic Conflict & Escalation Monitor — 16 May 2026
item.description: Russia conducted its deadliest civilian strike wave of 2026 in the days preceding the Trump-brokered 9-11 May Victory Day ceasefire, killing at least 73 civilians and injuring over 400 across eastern

item.title: Artificial Intelligence Monitor — 16 May 2026
item.description: The EU AI Omnibus political agreement postponing high-risk AI system compliance to 2027-2028 is a structural admission that the EU standardisation machinery cannot deliver harmonised standards at the

item.title: Global Macro Monitor — 16 May 2026
item.description: The USTR Section 301 legal architecture shift represents a regime-level structural hardening of the tariff regime, not a tactical escalation. The effective tariff rate baseline is likely to rise post-

item.title: Global Environmental Risks Monitor — 16 May 2026
item.description: Coral tipping system has entered regime where bleaching recurrence intervals are shorter than recovery timescales, indicating accelerating approach to irreversible collapse threshold

item.title: European Strategic Autonomy Monitor — 15 May 2026
item.description: EEAS 4th FIMI Threat Report operationalises EU deterrence posture with Galaxy Explorer dashboard and infrastructure disruption playbook

item.title: European Strategic Autonomy Monitor — W/E 14 May 2026
item.description: EEAS 4th FIMI Threat Report confirms Russian information warfare infrastructure pivoting from Moldova to Armenia ahead of June 2026 elections

item.title: FIMI & Cognitive Warfare Monitor — W/E 14 May 2026
item.description: No new FIMI operations disclosed this week — platform reporting cadence gaps persist across all Tier 1 sources

item.title: European Strategic Autonomy Monitor — W/E 13 May 2026
item.description: US withdraws from counter-FIMI leadership as Russian hybrid operations escalate across EU territory

item.title: European Strategic Autonomy Monitor — W/E 12 May 2026
item.description: EU gas storage at 30% entering May 2026, critically below 90% November target, as Strait of Hormuz disruptions expose structural energy dependency

item.title: Strategic Conflict & Escalation Monitor — W/E 12 May 2026
item.description: Israel expands territorial control to 58% of Gaza as Hamas rejects disarmament; April deadliest month of 2026

item.title: Artificial Intelligence Monitor — W/E 12 May 2026
item.description: The EU Digital Omnibus package represents a strategic political decision to prioritise industry readiness over regulatory enforcement, creating a 16-24 month window where high-risk AI systems can be d

item.title: FIMI & Cognitive Warfare Monitor — W/E 12 May 2026
item.description: Russia deploys Moldova FIMI playbook against Armenia ahead of June 2026 elections, per EEAS 4th Threat Report

item.title: Global Macro Monitor — W/E 12 May 2026
item.description: The USTR's launch of 76 Section 301 investigations represents a regime-level legal re-architecture of the US tariff regime, not a marginal escalation event. Section 301 is harder to challenge in court

item.title: World Democracy Monitor — W/E 12 May 2026
item.description: Georgia third foreign-funding law package combined with criminal prosecution of eight opposition leaders and Constitutional Court petition targeting entire nine-party opposition alliance represents th

item.title: Global Environmental Risks Monitor — W/E 12 May 2026
item.description: Three-year average 2023-2025 exceeding 1.5C represents structural acceleration of climate change boundary transgression, not single-year anomaly. Projected sustained breach by February 2034 is nine ye

item.description: EU gas storage at 30% entering May 2026, critically below 90% November target, as Strait of Hormuz disruptions expose structural energy dependency

item.description: The USTR's launch of 76 Section 301 investigations represents a regime-level legal re-architecture of the US tariff regime, not a marginal escalation event. Section 301 is harder to challenge in court

item.title: European Strategic Autonomy Monitor — W/E 5 May 2026
item.description: EU gas storage at 30% entering May 2026, well below 90% November target, as Hormuz disruptions expose structural energy dependency

item.title: Strategic Conflict & Escalation Monitor — W/E 5 May 2026
item.description: Sudan civil war enters multi-front expansion phase as RSF and SPLM-N advance on Blue Nile capital Damazin while SAF targets RSF gold mines and resumes manned airstrikes in Kordofan

item.title: FIMI & Cognitive Warfare Monitor — W/E 5 May 2026
item.description: Russia deploys Moldova FIMI playbook against Armenia ahead of June 2026 elections, per EEAS 4th Threat Report

item.title: Global Macro Monitor — W/E 5 May 2026
item.description: The Federal Reserve is locked into a stagflationary policy trap: tariff-driven goods inflation prevents easing, while tariff-driven growth drag prevents tightening. This is policy paralysis, not a neu

item.title: World Democracy Monitor — W/E 5 May 2026
item.description: The criminalisation of journalism represents a qualitative shift in press freedom suppression mechanisms globally, enabling regimes to maintain electoral autocracy classifications while suppressing pr

item.title: Global Environmental Risks Monitor — W/E 5 May 2026
item.description: Three-year average 2023-2025 exceeding 1.5C represents structural acceleration of climate change boundary transgression, not single-year anomaly. Projected sustained breach by February 2034 is nine ye

item.title: Artificial Intelligence Monitor — W/E 2 May 2026
item.description: OpenAI released GPT-5.5 on 2026-04-23, marking a significant advancement in agentic AI capabilities with enhanced coding and multi-tool task execution. This release coincides with OpenAI diversifying

item.title: Global Environmental Risks Monitor — W/E 2 May 2026
item.description: AMOC trajectory shows acceleration across three independent Tier 2 studies

item.title: Artificial Intelligence Monitor — W/E 1 May 2026
item.description: OpenAI released GPT-5.5 on 2026-04-23, marking a significant advancement in agentic AI capabilities with enhanced coding and multi-tool task execution. This release coincides with OpenAI diversifying

item.title: Strategic Conflict & Escalation Monitor — W/E 30 April 2026
item.description: JNIM-Tuareg forces strike Mali capital Bamako killing Defense Minister in major Sahel escalation

item.title: FIMI & Cognitive Warfare Monitor — W/E 30 April 2026
item.description: No new FIMI operations identified this week

item.title: Global Environmental Risks Monitor — W/E 30 April 2026
item.description: AMOC weakening trajectory accelerated 60% beyond model projections

item.title: European Strategic Autonomy Monitor — W/E 29 April 2026
item.description: EU sanctions Russian FIMI entities Euromore and Pravfond for influence operations

item.title: World Democracy Monitor — W/E 27 April 2026
item.description: United States has crossed threshold into electoral democracy status with structural risks to multiple democratic pillars

item.title: Artificial Intelligence Monitor — W/E 24 April 2026 (Re-issued)
item.description: Re-issued 2026-04-24 per AD-2026-04-24l-instance-1. Original publish shipped 5 developments; K0-pass2 lab evidence produced 14.

item.title: Artificial Intelligence Monitor — W/E 21 April 2026
item.description: The EU AI Act standards vacuum represents the most immediate governance risk as high-risk compliance deadline approaches without sufficient harmonised standards

item.title: Global Macro Monitor — W/E 21 April 2026
item.description: Tariff shock has transitioned from uncertainty to structural competitiveness drag

item.title: World Democracy Monitor — W/E 20 April 2026
item.description: Hungary exemplifies autocratisation from within via institutional capture

item.title: Strategic Conflict & Escalation Monitor — W/E 19 April 2026
item.description: Direct military exchanges between Iran-US-Israel on Apr 19 with Houthi Red Sea threats risk regional widening

item.title: Strategic Conflict & Escalation Monitor — W/E 18 April 2026
item.description: Hormuz closure constitutes largest oil disruption risk amid sustained I2/I4 Red levels despite F3/F4 flags

item.title: World Democracy Monitor — W/E 18 April 2026
item.description: US faces rapid institutional decay with vdem_ert_active across multiple pillars

item.title: Global Environmental Risks Monitor — W/E 18 April 2026
item.description: Six planetary boundaries remain transgressed with no incremental shifts this week per Tier 1 confirmation

item.title: Global Macro Monitor — W/E 17 April 2026
item.description: The Iran war and closure of the Strait of Hormuz represents the most significant supply shock to the global oil market in history, with 20% of global supply disrupted
